title: AI vs. Traditional In-Store Music — Entuned Blog
meta_description: How AI-powered retail music compares to Mood Media, SiriusXM Business, and Soundtrack Your Brand on targeting, measurement, and licensing.

faq.q: Where does AI-generated retail music come from compared to catalog-based services?
faq.a: Traditional providers license existing recordings and build playlists from them. AI systems like Entuned generate original music from scratch to specification, with no catalog, no repetition, and no licensing fees.

faq.q: How does psychographic targeting differ from traditional playlist selection?
faq.a: Traditional services target by genre or mood. Entuned translates psychographic customer data into 31 musical parameters — tempo, harmonic density, timbral warmth — generating music precisely matched to your customer profile.

faq.q: Does AI-generated music repeat like a playlist?
faq.a: No. Generative systems produce a continuous, non-repeating soundtrack. Musical parameters stay consistent for behavioral targeting, but each composition is new, eliminating repetition fatigue for both employees and frequent customers.

faq.q: How do licensing economics compare between traditional and AI music services?
faq.a: Traditional providers embed PRO licensing fees (ASCAP, BMI, SESAC) in their pricing, totaling $50K–$90K annually for a 50-location retailer. Entuned generates original music with no licensing fees, with pricing of $299–$599 per location per month.

faq.q: What should retailers evaluate when choosing between traditional and AI-powered music?
faq.a: The key question is whether you intend to measure audio's impact on business outcomes. If yes, catalog-based systems cannot deliver — they provide no parameterized data to optimize against. AI-powered systems enable closed-loop measurement and optimization.
